title: Sphaerocionium dilatatum
taxonomy: Sphaerocionium dilatatum belongs to the family Sphaerocionitaceae. It is classified as a species of fern in the division Pteridophyta and class Polypodiopsida. The genus Sphaerocionium is a small group of liverworts, specifically within the family Sphaerocionitaceae. Sphaerocionium dilatatum is also classified as a species of liverwort in the phylum Marchantiophyta. Its classification further categorizes it as a member of the order Jungermanniales.

intro: Sphaerocionium dilatatum, also known as the Dilated Button Fern or Button Fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Sphaerocionium dilatatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sphaerocionium dilatatum is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
